He claimed the well did not merely hold water; it answered wishes when spoken in a low, patient voice.

The wind spoke in a whisper, telling Amara to listen to the ground beneath her feet and to plant a circle of willow seeds around the well. The seeds would wake only when a true desire was spoken without fear.

Amara followed, and over many days she tended the circle. The wind returned with rain, not thunder, and the drought fled. The village learned that listening to the earth can turn fear into mercy and rain.
